translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In [the] beginning was the Word, and the Word was withGod, and the Word wasGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He was in the beginning withGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All things received being through him, and without him not one [thing] received being which has received being.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In him was life, and the life was the light of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the light appears in darkness, and the darkness apprehended it not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was a man sent fromGod, his name John.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He came for witness, that he might witness concerning the light, that all might believe through him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He was not the light, but that he might witness concerning the light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The true light was that which, coming into the world, lightens every man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He was in the world, and the world had [its] being through him, and the world knew him not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He came to his own, and his own received him not;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but as many as received him, to them gave he [the] right to be children ofGod, to those that believe on his name;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who have been born, not of blood, nor of flesh's will, nor of man's will, but ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Word became flesh, and dwelt among us (and we have contemplated his glory, a glory as of an only-begotten with a father), full of grace and truth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (John bears witness of him, and he has cried, saying, This was he of whom I said, He that comes after me is preferred before me, for he was before me;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for of his fulness we all have received, and grace upon grace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the law was given by Moses: grace and truth subsists through Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No one has seenGod at any time; the only-begotten Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the witness of John, when the Jews sent from Jerusalem priests and Levites that they might ask him, Thou, who art thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he acknowledged and denied not, and acknowledged, I am not the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they asked him, What then? Art thou Elias? And he says, I am not. Art thou the prophet? And he answered, No.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said therefore to him, Who art thou? that we may give an answer to those who sent us. What sayest thou of thyself?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He said, I [am] [the] voice of one crying in the wilderness, Make straight the path of [the] Lord, as said Esaias the prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were sent from among the Pharisees.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they asked him and said to him, Why baptisest thou then, if thou art not the Christ, nor Elias, nor the prophet?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: John answered them saying, I baptise with water. In the midst of you stands, whom ye do not know,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he who comes after me, the thong of whose sandal I am not worthy to unloose.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things took place in Bethany, across the Jordan, where John was baptising.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the morrow he sees Jesus coming to him, and says, Behold the Lamb ofGod, who takes away the sin of the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He it is of whom I said, A man comes after me who takes a place before me, because he was before me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I knew him not; but that he might be manifested to Israel, therefore have I come baptising with water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And John bore witness, saying, I beheld the Spirit descending as a dove from heaven, and it abode upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I knew him not; but he who sent me to baptise with water, he said to me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending and abiding on him, he it is who baptises with [the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have seen and borne witness that this is the Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again, on the morrow, there stood John and two of his disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, looking at Jesus as he walked, he says, Behold the Lamb ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the two disciples heard him speaking, and followed Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus having turned, and seeing them following, says to them, What seek ye? And they said to him, Rabbi (which, being interpreted, signifies Teacher), where abidest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He says to them, Come and see. They went therefore, and saw where he abode; and they abode with him that day. It was about the tenth hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the two who heard [this] from John and followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He first finds his own brother Simon, and says to him, We have found the Messias (which being interpreted is Christ).

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he led him to Jesus. Jesus looking at him said, Thou art Simon, the son of Jonas; thou shalt be called Cephas (which interpreted is stone).

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the morrow he would go forth into Galilee, and Jesus finds Philip, and says to him, Follow me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Philip finds Nathanael, and says to him, We have found him of whom Moses wrote in the law, and the prophets, Jesus, the son of Joseph, who is from Nazareth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nathanael said to him, Can anything good come out of Nazareth? Philip says to him, Come and see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and says of him, Behold [one] truly an Israelite, in whom there is no guile.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nathanael says to him, Whence knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said to him, Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig-tree, I saw thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nathanael answered and said to him, Rabbi, thou art the Son ofGod, thou art the King of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to him, Because I said to thee, I saw thee under the fig-tree, believest thou? Thou shalt see greater things than these.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to him, Verily, verily, I say to you, Henceforth ye shall see the heaven opened, and the angels ofGod ascending and descending on the Son of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the third day a marriage took place in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus also, and his disciples, were invited to the marriage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And wine being deficient, the mother of Jesus says to him, They have no wine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to her, What have I to do with thee, woman? mine hour has not yet come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His mother says to the servants, Whatever he may say to you, do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there were standing there six stone water-vessels, according to the purification of the Jews, holding two or three measures each.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to them, Fill the water-vessels with water. And they filled them up to the brim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, Draw out now, and carry [it] to the feast-master. And they carried [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the feast-master had tasted the water which had been made wine (and knew not whence it was, but the servants knew who drew the water), the feast-master calls the bridegroom,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and says to him, Every man sets on first the good wine, and when [men] have well drunk, then the inferior; thou hast kept the good wine till now.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This beginning of signs did Jesus in Cana of Galilee, and manifested his glory; and his disciples believed on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After this he descended to Capernaum, he and his mother and his brethren and his disciples; and there they abode not many days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the passover of the Jews was near, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he found in the temple the sellers of oxen and sheep and doves, and the money-changers sitting;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, having made a scourge of cords, he cast [them] all out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and he poured out the change of the money-changers, and overturned the tables,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to the sellers of doves, Take these things hence; make not my Father's house a house of merchandise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [And] his disciples remembered that it is written, The zeal of thy house devours me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore answered and said to him, What sign shewest thou to us, that thou doest these things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to them, Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore said, Forty and six years was this temple building, and thou wilt raise it up in three days?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he spoke of the temple of his body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore he was raised from among [the] dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this, and believed the scripture and the word which Jesus had spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was in Jerusalem, at the passover, at the feast, many believed on his name, beholding his signs which he wrought.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus himself did not trust himself to them, because he knew all [men],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and that he had not need that any should testify of man, for himself knew what was in man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there was a man from among the Pharisees, his name Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he came to him by night, and said to him, Rabbi, we know that thou art come a teacher fromGod, for none can do these signs that thou doest unlessGod be with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except any one be born anew he cannot see the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nicodemus says to him, How can a man be born being old? can he enter a second time into the womb of his mother and be born?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except any one be born of water and of Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do not wonder that I said to thee, It is needful that ye should be born anew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wind blows where it will, and thou hearest its voice, but knowest not whence it comes and where it goes: thus is every one that is born of the Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nicodemus answered and said to him, How can these things be?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to him, Thou art the teacher of Israel and knowest not these things!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that which we know, and we bear witness of that which we have seen, and ye receive not our witness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have said the earthly things to you, and ye believe not, how, if I say the heavenly things to you, will ye believe?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And no one has gone up into heaven, save he who came down out of heaven, the Son of man who is in heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, thus must the Son of man be lifted up,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that every one who believes on him may [not perish, but] have life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod so loved the world, that he gave his only-begotten Son, that whosoever believes on him may not perish, but have life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod has not sent his Son into the world that he may judge the world, but that the world may be saved through him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that believes on him is not judged: but he that believes not has been already judged, because he has not believed on the name of the only-begotten Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the judgment, that light is come into the world, and men have loved darkness rather than light; for their works were evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every one that does evil hates the light, and does not come to the light that his works may not be shewn as they are;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he that practises the truth comes to the light, that his works may be manifested that they have been wrought inGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After these things came Jesus and his disciples into the land of Judaea; and there he abode with them and baptised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And John also was baptising in Aenon, near Salim, because there was a great deal of water there; and they came to [him] and were baptised:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for John was not yet cast into prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was therefore a reasoning of the disciples of John with a Jew about purification.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came to John and said to him, Rabbi, he who was with thee beyond the Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold, he baptises, and all come to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: John answered and said, A man can receive nothing unless it be given him out of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye yourselves bear me witness that I said, I am not the Christ, but, that I am sent before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices in heart because of the voice of the bridegroom: this my joy then is fulfilled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He must increase, but I must decrease.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He who comes from above is above all. He who has his origin in the earth is of the earth, and speaks [as] of the earth. He who comes out of heaven is above all,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [and] what he has seen and has heard, this he testifies; and no one receives his testimony.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that has received his testimony has set to his seal thatGod is true;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he whomGod has sent speaks the words ofGod, forGod gives not the Spirit by measure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Father loves the Son, and has given all things [to be] in his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that believes on the Son has life eternal, and he that is not subject to the Son shall not see life, but the wrath ofGod abides upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore the Lord knew that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus makes and baptises more disciples than John

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (however, Jesus himself did not baptise, but his disciples),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he left Judaea and went away again unto Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he must needs pass through Samaria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He comes therefore to a city of Samaria called Sychar, near to the land which Jacob gave to his son Joseph.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now a fountain of Jacob's was there; Jesus therefore, being wearied with the way he had come, sat just as he was at the fountain. It was about the sixth hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A woman comes out of Samaria to draw water. Jesus says to her, Give me to drink

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for his disciples had gone away into the city that they might buy provisions).

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Samaritan woman therefore says to him, How dost thou, being a Jew, ask to drink of me who am a Samaritan woman? for Jews have no intercourse with Samaritans.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to her, If thou knewest the gift ofGod, and who it is that says to thee, Give me to drink, thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The woman says to him, Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: whence then hast thou the living water?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Art thou greater than our father Jacob, who gave us the well, and drank of it himself, and his sons, and his cattle?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to her, Every one who drinks of this water shall thirst again;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but whosoever drinks of the water which I shall give him shall never thirst for ever, but the water which I shall give him shall become in him a fountain of water, springing up into eternal life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The woman says to him, Sir, give me this water, that I may not thirst nor come here to draw.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to her, Go, call thy husband, and come here.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The woman answered and said, I have not a husband. Jesus says to her, Thou hast well said, I have not a husband;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for thou hast had five husbands, and he whom now thou hast is not thy husband: this thou hast spoken truly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The woman says to him, Sir, I see that thou art a prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our fathers worshipped in this mountain, and ye say that in Jerusalem is the place where one must worship.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to her, Woman, believe me, [the] hour is coming when ye shall neither in this mountain nor in Jerusalem worship the Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye worship ye know not what; we worship what we know, for salvation is of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But [the] hour is coming and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and truth; for also the Father seeks such as his worshippers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God [is] a spirit; and they who worship him must worship [him] in spirit and truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The woman says to him, I know that Messias is coming, who is called Christ; when he comes he will tell us all things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to her, I who speak to thee am [he].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And upon this came his disciples, and wondered that he spoke with a woman; yet no one said, What seekest thou? or, Why speakest thou with her?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The woman then left her waterpot and went away into the city, and says to the men,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come, see a man who told me all things I had ever done: is not he the Christ?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They went out of the city and came to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But meanwhile the disciples asked him saying, Rabbi, eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said to them, I have food to eat which ye do not know.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The disciples therefore said to one another, Has any one brought him [anything] to eat?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to them, My food is that I should do the will of him that has sent me, and that I should finish his work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do not ye say, that there are yet four months and the harvest comes? Behold, I say to you, Lift up your eyes and behold the fields, for they are already white to harvest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that reaps receives wages and gathers fruit unto life eternal, that both he that sows and he that reaps may rejoice together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in this is [verified] the true saying, It is one who sows and another who reaps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have sent you to reap that on which ye have not laboured; others have laboured, and ye have entered into their labours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But many of the Samaritans of that city believed on him because of the word of the woman who bore witness, He told me all things that I had ever done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore the Samaritans came to him they asked him to abide with them, and he abode there two days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And more a great deal believed on account of his word;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they said to the woman, [It is] no longer on account of thy saying that we believe, for we have heard him ourselves, and we know that this is indeed the Saviour of the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But after the two days he went forth thence and went away into Galilee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Jesus himself bore witness that a prophet has no honour in his own country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore he came into Galilee, the Galileans received him, having seen all that he had done in Jerusalem during the feast, for they also went to the feast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He came therefore again to Cana of Galilee, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain courtier in Capernaum whose son was sick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He, having heard that Jesus had come out of Judaea into Galilee, went to him and asked [him] that he would come down and heal his son, for he was about to die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said to him, Unless ye see signs and wonders ye will not believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The courtier says to him, Sir, come down ere my child die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to him, Go, thy son lives. And the man believed the word which Jesus said to him, and went his way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But already, as he was going down, his servants met him and brought [him] word saying, Thy child lives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He inquired therefore from them the hour at which he got better. And they said to him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The father therefore knew that [it was] in that hour in which Jesus said to him, Thy son lives; and he believed, himself and his whole house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This second sign again did Jesus, being come out of Judaea into Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After these things was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there is in Jerusalem, at the sheepgate, a pool, which is called in Hebrew, Bethesda, having five porches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In these lay a multitude of sick, blind, lame, withered, [awaiting the moving of the water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For an angel descended at a certain season in the pool and troubled the water. Whoever therefore first went in after the troubling of the water became well, whatever disease he laboured under.]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there was a certain man there who had been suffering under his infirmity thirty and eight years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus seeing this [man] lying [there], and knowing that he was [in that state] now a great length of time, says to him, Wouldest thou become well?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The infirm [man] answered him, Sir, I have not a man, in order, when the water has been troubled, to cast me into the pool; but while I am coming another descends before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to him, Arise, take up thy couch and walk.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately the man became well, and took up his couch and walked: and on that day was sabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore said to the healed [man], It is sabbath, it is not permitted thee to take up thy couch.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He answered them, He that made me well, he said to me, Take up thy couch and walk.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They asked him [therefore], Who is the man who said to thee, Take up thy couch and walk?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he that had been healed knew not who it was, for Jesus had slidden away, there being a crowd in the place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After these things Jesus finds him in the temple, and said to him, Behold, thou art become well: sin no more, that something worse do not happen to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for this the Jews persecuted Jesus [and sought to kill him], because he had done these things on sabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus answered them, My Father worketh hitherto and I work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him, because he had not only violated the sabbath, but also said thatGod was his own Father, making himself equal withGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore answered and said to them, Verily, verily, I say to you, The Son can do nothing of himself save whatever he sees the Father doing: for whatever things he does, these things also the Son does in like manner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the Father loves the Son and shews him all things which he himself does; and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may wonder.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For even as the Father raises the dead and quickens [them], thus the Son also quickens whom he will:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for neither does the Father judge any one, but has given all judgment to the Son;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that all may honour the Son, even as they honour the Father. He who honours not the Son, honours not the Father who has sent him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say unto you, that he that hears my word, and believes him that has sent me, has life eternal, and does not come into judgment, but is passed out of death into life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say unto you, that an hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son ofGod, and they that have heard shall live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For even as the Father has life in himself, so he has given to the Son also to have life in himself,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and has given him authority to execute judgment [also], because he is Son of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wonder not at this, for an hour is coming in which all who are in the tombs shall hear his voice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shall go forth; those that have practised good, to resurrection of life, and those that have done evil, to resurrection of judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I cannot do anything of myself; as I hear, I judge, and my judgment is righteous, because I do not seek my will, but the will of him that has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I bear witness concerning myself, my witness is not true.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is another who bears witness concerning me, and I know that the witness which he bears concerning me is true.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have sent unto John, and he has borne witness to the truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I do not receive witness from man, but I say this that ye might be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He was the burning and shining lamp, and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I have the witness [that is] greater than [that] of John; for the works which the Father has given me that I should complete them, the works themselves which I do, bear witness concerning me that the Father has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Father who has sent me himself has borne witness concerning me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor have seen his shape,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye have not his word abiding in you; for whom he hath sent, him ye do not believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye search the scriptures, for ye think that in them ye have life eternal, and they it is which bear witness concerning me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye will not come to me that ye might have life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I do not receive glory from men,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I know you, that ye have not the love ofGod in you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am come in my Father's name, and ye receive me not; if another come in his own name, him ye will receive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How can ye believe, who receive glory one of another, and seek not the glory which [comes] fromGod alone?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Think not that I will accuse you to the Father: there is [one] who accuses you, Moses, on whom ye trust;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for if ye had believed Moses, ye would have believed me, for he wrote of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye do not believe his writings, how shall ye believe my words?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After these things Jesus went away beyond the sea of Galilee, [or] of Tiberias,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and a great crowd followed him, because they saw the signs which he wrought upon the sick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus went up into the mountain, and there sat with his disciples:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the passover, the feast of the Jews, was near.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus then, lifting up his eyes and seeing that a great crowd is coming to him, says to Philip, Whence shall we buy loaves that these may eat?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this he said trying him, for he knew what he was going to do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Philip answered him, Loaves for two hundred denarii are not sufficient for them, that each may have some little [portion].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, says to him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is a little boy here who has five barley loaves and two small fishes; but this, what is it for so many?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [And] Jesus said, Make the men sit down. Now there was much grass in the place: the men therefore sat down, in number about five thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus took the loaves, and having given thanks, distributed [them] to those that were set down; and in like manner of the small fishes as much as they would.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had been filled, he says to his disciples, Gather together the fragments which are over and above, that nothing may be lost.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They gathered [them] therefore together, and filled twelve hand-baskets full of fragments of the five barley loaves, which were over and above to those that had eaten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The men therefore, having seen the sign which Jesus had done, said, This is truly the prophet which is coming into the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore knowing that they were going to come and seize him, that they might make [him] king, departed again to the mountain himself alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when evening was come, his disciples went down to the sea,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having gone on board ship, they went over the sea to Capernaum. And it had already become dark, and Jesus had not come to them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the sea was agitated by a strong wind blowing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having rowed then about twenty-five or thirty stadia, they see Jesus walking on the sea and coming near the ship; and they were frightened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he says to them, It is I: be not afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They were willing therefore to receive him into the ship; and immediately the ship was at the land to which they went.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the morrow the crowd which stood on the other side of the sea, having seen that there was no other little ship there except that into which his disciples had got, and that Jesus had not gone with his disciples into the ship, but [that] his disciples had gone away alone;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (but other little ships out of Tiberias came near to the place where they ate bread after the Lord had given thanks;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: when therefore the crowd saw that Jesus was not there, nor his disciples, they got into the ships, and came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having found him the other side of the sea, they said to him, Rabbi, when art thou arrived here?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them and said, Verily, verily, I say to you, Ye seek me not because ye have seen signs, but because ye have eaten of the loaves and been filled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Work not [for] the food which perishes, but [for] the food which abides unto life eternal, which the Son of man shall give to you; for him has the Father sealed, [even]God.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said therefore to him, What should we do that we may work the works ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to them, This is the work ofGod, that ye believe on him whom he has sent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said therefore to him, What sign then doest thou that we may see and believe thee? what dost thou work?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, as it is written, He gave them bread out of heaven to eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said to them, Verily, verily, I say to you, [It is] not Moses that has given you the bread out of heaven; but my Father gives you the true bread out of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the bread ofGod is he who comes down out of heaven and gives life to the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said therefore to him, Lord, ever give to us this bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [And] Jesus said to them, I am the bread of life: he that comes to me shall never hunger, and he that believes on me shall never thirst at any time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I have said to you, that ye have also seen me and do not believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that the Father gives me shall come to me, and him that comes to me I will not at all cast out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I am come down from heaven, not that I should do my will, but the will of him that has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the will of him that has sent me, that of all that he has given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up in the last day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this is the will of my Father, that every one who sees the Son, and believes on him, should have life eternal; and I will raise him up at the last day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore murmured about him, because he said, I am the bread which has come down out of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Is not this Jesus the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we have known? how then does he say, I am come down out of heaven?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore answered and said to them, Murmur not among yourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No one can come to me except the Father who has sent me draw him, and I will raise him up in the last day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught ofGod. Every one that has heard from the Father [himself], and has learned [of him], comes to me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not that any one has seen the Father, except he who is ofGod, he has seen the Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say to you, He that believes [on me] has life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am the bread of life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness and died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the bread which comes down out of heaven, that one may eat of it and not die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am the living bread which has come down out of heaven: if any one shall have eaten of this bread he shall live for ever; but the bread withal which I shall give is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore contended among themselves, saying, How can he give us this flesh to eat?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said to them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Unless ye shall have eaten the flesh of the Son of man, and drunk his blood, ye have no life in yourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that eats my flesh and drinks my blood has life eternal, and I will raise him up at the last day:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for my flesh is truly food and my blood is truly drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that eats my flesh and drinks my blood dwells in me and I in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As the living Father has sent me and I live on account of the Father, he also who eats me shall live also on account of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the bread which has come down out of heaven. Not as the fathers ate and died: he that eats this bread shall live for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things he said in [the] synagogue, teaching in Capernaum.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many therefore of his disciples having heard [it] said, This word is hard; who can hear it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, knowing in himself that his disciples murmur concerning this, said to them, Does this offend you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If then ye see the Son of man ascending up where he was before?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is the Spirit which quickens, the flesh profits nothing: the words which I have spoken unto you are spirit and are life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there are some of you who do not believe. For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who did not believe, and who would deliver him up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Therefore said I unto you, that no one can come to me unless it be given to him from the Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From that [time] many of his disciples went away back and walked no more with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said to the twelve, Will ye also go away?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon Peter answered him, Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast words of life eternal;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and we have believed and known that thou art the holy one ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, Have not I chosen you the twelve? and of you one is a devil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he spoke of Judas [the son] of Simon, Iscariote, for he [it was who] should deliver him up, being one of the twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after these things Jesus walked in Galilee, for he would not walk in Judaea, because the Jews sought to kill him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the tabernacles, the feast of the Jews, was near.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His brethren therefore said to him, Remove hence and go into Judaea, that thy disciples also may see thy works which thou doest;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for no one does anything in secret and himself seeks to be [known] in public. If thou doest these things, manifest thyself to the world:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for neither did his brethren believe on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore says to them, My time is not yet come, but your time is always ready.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The world cannot hate you, but me it hates, because I bear witness concerning it that its works are evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye, go ye up to this feast. I go not up to this feast, for my time is not yet fulfilled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having said these things to them he abode in Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when his brethren had gone up, then he himself also went up to the feast, not openly, but as in secret.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore sought him at the feast, and said, Where is he?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was much murmuring concerning him among the crowds. Some said, He is [a] good [man]; others said, No; but he deceives the crowd.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: However, no one spoke openly concerning him on account of [their] fear of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when it was now the middle of the feast, Jesus went up into the temple and taught.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore wondered, saying, How knows this [man] letters, having never learned?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore answered them and said, My doctrine is not mine, but [that] of him that has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one desire to practise his will, he shall know concerning the doctrine, whether it is ofGod, or [that] I speak from myself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that speaks from himself seeks his own glory; but he that seeks the glory of him that has sent him, he is true, and unrighteousness is not in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Has not Moses given you the law, and no one of you practises the law? Why do ye seek to kill me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The crowd answered [and said], Thou hast a demon: who seeks to kill thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to them, I have done one work, and ye all wonder.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore Moses gave you circumcision (not that it is of Moses, but of the fathers), and ye circumcise a man on sabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If a man receives circumcision on sabbath, that the law of Moses may not be violated, are ye angry with me because I have made a man entirely sound on sabbath?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judge not according to sight, but judge righteous judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Some therefore of those of Jerusalem said, Is not this he whom they seek to kill?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and behold, he speaks openly, and they say nothing to him. Have the rulers then indeed recognised that this is the Christ?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But [as to] this [man] we know whence he is. Now [as to] the Christ, when he comes, no one knows whence he is.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore cried out in the temple, teaching and saying, Ye both know me and ye know whence I am; and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye do not know.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I know him, because I am from him, and he has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They sought therefore to take him; and no one laid his hand upon him, because his hour had not yet come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But many of the crowd believed on him, and said, Will the Christ, when he comes, do more signs than those which this [man] has done?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees heard the crowd murmuring these things concerning him, and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers that they might take him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said, Yet a little while I am with you, and I go to him that has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye shall seek me and shall not find [me], and where I am ye cannot come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore said to one another, Where is he about to go that we shall not find him? Is he about to go to the dispersion among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What word is this which he said, Ye shall seek me and shall not find [me]; and where I am ye cannot come?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the last, the great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried saying, If any one thirst, let him come to me and drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that believes on me, as the scripture has said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this he said concerning the Spirit, which they that believed on him were about to receive; for [the] Spirit was not yet, because Jesus had not yet been glorified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Some] out of the crowd therefore, having heard this word, said, This is truly the prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Others said, This is the Christ. Others said, Does then the Christ come out of Galilee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Has not the scripture said that the Christ comes of the seed of David, and from the village of Bethlehem, where David was?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was a division therefore in the crowd on account of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But some of them desired to take him, but no one laid hands upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The officers therefore came to the chief priests and Pharisees, and they said to them, Why have ye not brought him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The officers answered, Never man spoke thus, as this man [speaks].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees therefore answered them, Are ye also deceived?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Has any one of the rulers believed on him, or of the Pharisees?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this crowd, which does not know the law, are accursed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nicodemus says to them (being one of themselves),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Does our law judge a man before it have first heard from himself, and know what he does?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They answered and said to him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search and look, that no prophet arises out of Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every one went to his home.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus went to the mount of Olives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came to him; and he sat down and taught them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribes and the Pharisees bring [to him] a woman taken in adultery, and having set her in the midst,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they say to him, Teacher, this woman has been taken in the very act, committing adultery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now in the law Moses has commanded us to stone such; thou therefore, what sayest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this they said proving him, that they might have [something] to accuse him [of]. But Jesus, having stooped down, wrote with his finger on the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when they continued asking him, he lifted himself up and said to them, Let him that is without sin among you first cast the stone at her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again stooping down he wrote on the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they, having heard [that], went out one by one beginning from the elder ones until the last; and Jesus was left alone and the woman standing there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, lifting himself up and seeing no one but the woman, said to her, Woman, where are those thine accusers? Has no one condemned thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, No one, sir. And Jesus said to her, Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again therefore Jesus spoke to them, saying, I am the light of the world; he that follows me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees therefore said to him, Thou bearest witness concerning thyself; thy witness is not true.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to them, Even if I bear witness concerning myself, my witness is true, because I know whence I came and whither I go: but ye know not whence I come and whither I go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye judge according to the flesh, I judge no one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if also I judge, my judgment is true, because I am not alone, but I and the Father who has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in your law too it is written that the testimony of two men is true:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am [one] who bear witness concerning myself, and the Father who has sent me bears witness concerning me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said to him therefore, Where is thy Father? Jesus answered, Ye know neither me nor my Father. If ye had known me, ye would have known also my Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These words spoke he in the treasury, teaching in the temple; and no one took him, for his hour was not yet come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He said therefore again to them, I go away, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your sin; where I go ye cannot come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore said, Will he kill himself, that he says, Where I go ye cannot come?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Ye are from beneath; I am from above. Ye are of this world; I am not of this world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I said therefore to you, that ye shall die in your sins; for unless ye shall believe that I am [he], ye shall die in your sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said therefore to him, Who art thou? [And] Jesus said to them, Altogether that which I also say to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have many things to say and to judge concerning you, but he that has sent me is true, and I, what I have heard from him, these things I say to the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They knew not that he spoke to them of the Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said to them, When ye shall have lifted up the Son of man, then ye shall know that I am [he], and [that] I do nothing of myself, but as the Father has taught me I speak these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that has sent me is with me; he has not left me alone, because I do always the things that are pleasing to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As he spoke these things many believed on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said to the Jews who believed him, If ye abide in my word, ye are truly my disciples;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall set you free.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They answered him, We are Abraham's seed, and have never been under bondage to any one; how sayest thou, Ye shall become free?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, Verily, verily, I say to you, Every one that practises sin is the bondman of sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the bondman abides not in the house for ever: the son abides for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore the Son shall set you free, ye shall be really free.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I know that ye are Abraham's seed; but ye seek to kill me, because my word has no entrance in you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I speak what I have seen with my Father, and ye then do what ye have seen with your father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They answered and said to him, Abraham is our father. Jesus says to them, If ye were Abraham's children, ye would do the works of Abraham;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but now ye seek to kill me, a man who has spoken the truth to you, which I have heard fromGod: this did not Abraham.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye do the works of your father. They said [therefore] to him, We are not born of fornication; we have one father,God.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, IfGod were your father ye would have loved me, for I came forth fromGod and am come [from him]; for neither am I come of myself, but he has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why do ye not know my speech? Because ye cannot hear my word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye are of the devil, as [your] father, and ye desire to do the lusts of your father. He was a murderer from the beginning, and has not stood in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks falsehood, he speaks of what is his own; for he is a liar and its father:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and because I speak the truth, ye do not believe me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which of you convinces me of sin? If I speak truth, why do ye not believe me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is ofGod hears the words ofGod: therefore ye hear [them] not, because ye are not ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews answered and said to him, Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan and hast a demon?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, I have not a demon; but I honour my Father, and ye dishonour me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I do not seek my own glory: there is he that seeks and judges.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say unto you, If any one shall keep my word, he shall never see death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore said to him, Now we know that thou hast a demon. Abraham has died, and the prophets, and thou sayest, If any one keep my word, he shall never taste death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Art thou greater than our father Abraham, who has died? and the prophets have died: whom makest thou thyself?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing: it is my Father who glorifies me, [of] whom ye say, He is ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye know him not; but I know him; and if I said, I know him not, I should be like you, a liar. But I know him, and I keep his word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Your father Abraham exulted in that he should see my day, and he saw and rejoiced.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore said to him, Thou hast not yet fifty years, and hast thou seen Abraham?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Before Abraham was, I am.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They took up therefore stones that they might cast [them] at him; but Jesus hid himself and went out of the temple, [going through the midst of them, and thus passed on.]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he passed on, he saw a man blind from birth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his disciples asked him, saying, Rabbi, who sinned, this [man] or his parents, that he should be born blind?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, Neither has this [man] sinned nor his parents, but that the works ofGod should be manifested in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I must work the works of him that has sent me while it is day. [The] night is coming, when no one can work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As long as I am in the world, I am [the] light of the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having said these things, he spat on the ground and made mud of the spittle, and put the mud, as ointment, on his eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Go, wash in the pool of Siloam, which is interpreted, Sent. He went therefore and washed, and came seeing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The neighbours therefore, and those who used to see him before, that he was a beggar, said, Is not this he that was sitting and begging?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Some said, It is he; others said, No, but he is like him: he said, It is I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said therefore to him, How have thine eyes been opened?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He answered [and said], A man called Jesus made mud and anointed mine eyes, and said to me, Go to Siloam and wash: and having gone and washed, I saw.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said therefore to him, Where is he? He says, I do not know.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They bring him who was before blind to the Pharisees.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now it was sabbath when Jesus made the mud and opened his eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees therefore also again asked him how he received his sight. And he said to them, He put mud upon mine eyes, and I washed, and I see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Some of the Pharisees therefore said, This man is not ofGod, for he does not keep the sabbath. Others said, How can a sinful man perform such signs? And there was a division among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say therefore again to the blind [man], What dost thou say of him, that he has opened thine eyes? And he said, He is a prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore did not believe concerning him that he was blind and had received sight, until they had called the parents of him that had received sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they asked them saying, This is your son, of whom ye say that he was born blind: how then does he now see?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His parents answered [them] and said, We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but how he now sees we do not know, or who has opened his eyes we do not know. He is of age: ask him; he will speak concerning himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His parents said these things because they feared the Jews, for the Jews had already agreed that if any one confessed him [to be the] Christ, he should be excommunicated from the synagogue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On this account his parents said, He is of age: ask him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They called therefore a second time the man who had been blind, and said to him, Give glory toGod: we know that this man is sinful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He answered therefore, If he is sinful I know not. One thing I know, that, being blind [before], now I see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him again, What did he do to thee? how opened he thine eyes?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He answered them, I told you already and ye did not hear: why do ye desire to hear again? do ye also wish to become his disciples?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They railed at him, and said, Thou art his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We know thatGod spoke to Moses; but [as to] this [man], we know not whence he is.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The man answered and said to them, Now in this is a wonderful thing, that ye do not know whence he is, and he has opened mine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [But] we know thatGod does not hear sinners; but if any one beGod-fearing and do his will, him he hears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since time was, it has not been heard that any one opened the eyes of one born blind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If this [man] were not ofGod he would be able to do nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They answered and said to him, Thou hast been wholly born in sins, and thou teachest us? And they cast him out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus heard that they had cast him out, and having found him, he said to him, Thou, dost thou believe on the Son ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He answered and said, And who is he, Lord, that I may believe on him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, Thou hast both seen him, and he that speaks with thee is he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I believe, Lord: and he did him homage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said, For judgment am I come into this world, that they which see not may see, and they which see may become blind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [some] of the Pharisees who were with him heard these things, and they said to him, Are we blind also?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, If ye were blind ye would not have sin; but now ye say, We see, your sin remains.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say to you, He that enters not in by the door to the fold of the sheep, but mounts up elsewhere, he is a thief and a robber;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he that enters in by the door is [the] shepherd of the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To him the porter opens; and the sheep hear his voice; and he calls his own sheep by name, and leads them out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When he has put forth all his own, he goes before them, and the sheep follow him, because they know his voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they will not follow a stranger, but will flee from him, because they know not the voice of strangers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This allegory spoke Jesus to them, but they did not know what it was [of] which he spoke to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said again to them, Verily, verily, I say to you, I am the door of the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All whoever came before me are thieves and robbers; but the sheep did not hear them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am the door: if any one enter in by me, he shall be saved, and shall go in and shall go out and shall find pasture.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The thief comes not but that he may steal, and kill, and destroy: I am come that they might have life, and might have [it] abundantly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he who serves for wages, and who is not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, sees the wolf coming, and leaves the sheep and flees; and the wolf seizes them and scatters the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he who serves for wages flees because he serves for wages, and is not himself concerned about the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am the good shepherd; and I know those that are mine, and am known of those that are mine,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as the Father knows me and I know the Father; and I lay down my life for the sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have other sheep which are not of this fold: those also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one flock, one shepherd.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On this account the Father loves me, because I lay down my life that I may take it again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have authority to lay it down and I have authority to take it again. I have received this commandment of my Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was a division again among the Jews on account of these words;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but many of them said, He has a demon and raves; why do ye hear him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Others said, These sayings are not [those] of one that is possessed by a demon. Can a demon open blind people's eyes?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the feast of the dedication was celebrating at Jerusalem, and it was winter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus walked in the temple in the porch of Solomon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore surrounded him, and said to him, Until when dost thou hold our soul in suspense? If thou art the Christ, say [so] to us openly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye do not believe. The works which I do in my Father's name, these bear witness concerning me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but ye do not believe, for ye are not of my sheep, as I told you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I give them life eternal; and they shall never perish, and no one shall seize them out of my hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My Father who has given [them] to me is greater than all, and no one can seize out of the hand of my Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I and the Father are one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore again took stones that they might stone him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewn you of my Father; for which work of them do ye stone me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews answered him, For a good work we stone thee not, but for blasphemy, and because thou, being a man, makest thyselfGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye aregods?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he called themgods to whom the word ofGod came (and the scripture cannot be broken),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: do ye say of him whom the Father has sanctified and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest, because I said, I am Son ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if I do, even if ye believe not me, believe the works, that ye may know [and believe] that the Father is in me and I in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They sought therefore again to take him; and he went away from out of their hand

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and departed again beyond the Jordan to the place where John was baptising at the first: and he abode there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And many came to him, and said, John did no sign; but all things which John said of this [man] were true.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And many believed on him there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there was a certain [man] sick, Lazarus of Bethany, of the village of Mary and Martha her sister.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It was [the] Mary who anointed the Lord with ointment and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sisters therefore sent to him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when Jesus heard [it], he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the glory ofGod, that the Son ofGod may be glorified by it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore he heard, He is sick, he remained two days then in the place where he was.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then after this he says to his disciples, Let us go into Judaea again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The disciples say to him, Rabbi, [even but] now the Jews sought to stone thee, and goest thou thither again?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any one walk in the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if any one walk in the night, he stumbles, because the light is not in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things said he; and after this he says to them, Lazarus, our friend, is fallen asleep, but I go that I may awake him out of sleep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The disciples therefore said to him, Lord, if he be fallen asleep, he will get well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus spoke of his death, but they thought that he spoke of the rest of sleep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore then said to them plainly, Lazarus has died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I rejoice on your account that I was not there, in order that ye may believe. But let us go to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thomas therefore, called Didymus, said to his fellow disciples, Let us also go, that we may die with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore [on] arriving found him to have been four days already in the tomb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about fifteen stadia off,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, that they might console them concerning their brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Martha then, when she heard Jesus is coming, went to meet him; but Mary sat in the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Martha therefore said to Jesus, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but even now I know, that whatsoever thou shalt ask ofGod,God will give thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to her, Thy brother shall rise again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Martha says to him, I know that he will rise again in the resurrection in the last day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to her, I am the resurrection and the life: he that believes on me, though he have died, shall live;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and every one who lives and believes on me shall never die. Believest thou this?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She says to him, Yea, Lord; I believe that thou art the Christ, the Son ofGod, who should come into the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having said this, she went away and called her sister Mary secretly, saying, The teacher is come and calls thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She, when she heard [that], rises up quickly and comes to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Jesus had not yet come into the village, but was in the place where Martha came to meet him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore who were with her in the house and consoling her, seeing Mary that she rose up quickly and went out, followed her, saying, She goes to the tomb, that she may weep there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mary therefore, when she came where Jesus was, seeing him, fell at his feet, saying to him, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore, when he saw her weeping, and the Jews who came with her weeping, was deeply moved in spirit, and was troubled,

translations.name: Darby Bible
