translations.text: and said, Where have ye put him? They say to him, Lord, come and see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus wept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore said, Behold how he loved him!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of them said, Could not this [man], who has opened the eyes of the blind [man], have caused that this [man] also should not have died?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore, again deeply moved in himself, comes to the tomb. Now it was a cave, and a stone lay upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says, Take away the stone. Martha, the sister of the dead, says to him, Lord, he stinks already, for he is four days [there].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to her, Did I not say to thee, that if thou shouldest believe, thou shouldest see the glory ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They took therefore the stone away. And Jesus lifted up his eyes on high and said, Father, I thank thee that thou hast heard me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I knew that thou always hearest me; but on account of the crowd who stand around I have said [it], that they may believe that thou hast sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having said this, he cried with a loud voice, Lazarus, come forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the dead came forth, bound feet and hands with graveclothes, and his face was bound round with a handkerchief. Jesus says to them, Loose him and let him go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many therefore of the Jews who came to Mary and saw what he had done, believed on him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The chief priests, therefore, and the Pharisees gathered a council, and said, What do we? for this man does many signs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If we let him thus alone, all will believe on him, and the Romans will come and take away both our place and our nation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a certain one of them, Caiaphas, being high priest that year, said to them, Ye know nothing

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor consider that it is profitable for you that one man die for the people, and not that the whole nation perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this he did not say of himself; but, being high priest that year, prophesied that Jesus was going to die for the nation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and not for the nation only, but that he should also gather together into one the children ofGod who were scattered abroad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From that day therefore they took counsel that they might kill him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore walked no longer openly among the Jews, but went away thence into the country near the desert, to a city called Ephraim, and there he sojourned with the disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the passover of the Jews was near, and many went up to Jerusalem out of the country before the passover, that they might purify themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They sought therefore Jesus, and said among themselves, standing in the temple, What do ye think? that he will not come to the feast?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the chief priests and the Pharisees had given commandment that if any one knew where he was, he should make it known, that they might take him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore, six days before the passover, came to Bethany, where was the dead [man] Lazarus, whom Jesus raised from among [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There therefore they made him a supper, and Martha served, but Lazarus was one of those at table with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mary therefore, having taken a pound of ointment of pure nard of great price, anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair, and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One of his disciples therefore, Judas [son] of Simon, Iscariote, who was about to deliver him up, says,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why was this ointment not sold for three hundred denarii and given to the poor?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said this, not that he cared for the poor, but because he was a thief and had the bag, and carried what was put into [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said, Suffer her to have kept this for the day of my preparation for burial;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for ye have the poor always with you, but me ye have not always.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A great crowd therefore of the Jews knew that he was there; and they came, not because of Jesus only, but also that they might see Lazarus whom he raised from among [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the chief priests took counsel that they might kill Lazarus also,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because many of the Jews went away on his account and believed on Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the morrow a great crowd who came to the feast, having heard that Jesus is coming into Jerusalem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: took branches of palms and went out to meet him, and cried, Hosanna, blessed [is] he that comes in the name of [the] Lord, the King of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, having found a young ass, sat upon it; as it is written,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear not, daughter of Zion: behold, thy King cometh, sitting on an ass's colt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Now] his disciples knew not these things at the first; but when Jesus was glorified, then they remembered that these things were written of him, and that they had done these things to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The crowd therefore that was with him bore witness because he had called Lazarus out of the tomb, and raised him from among [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore also the crowd met him because they had heard that he had done this sign.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees therefore said to one another, Ye see that ye profit nothing: behold, the world is gone after him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were certain Greeks among those who came up that they might worship in the feast;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: these therefore came to Philip, who was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and they asked him saying, Sir, we desire to see Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Philip comes and tells Andrew, [and again] Andrew comes and Philip, and they tell Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus answered them saying, The hour is come that the Son of man should be glorified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except the grain of wheat falling into the ground die, it abides alone; but if it die, it bears much fruit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that loves his life shall lose it, and he that hates his life in this world shall keep it to life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one serve me, let him follow me; and where I am, there also shall be my servant. [And] if any one serve me, him shall the Father honour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now is my soul troubled, and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour. But on account of this have I come to this hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Father, glorify thy name. There came therefore a voice out of heaven, I both have glorified and will glorify [it] again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The crowd therefore, which stood [there] and heard [it], said that it had thundered. Others said, An angel has spoken to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said, Not on my account has this voice come, but on yours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now is [the] judgment of this world; now shall the prince of this world be cast out:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I, if I be lifted up out of the earth, will draw all to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this he said signifying by what death he was about to die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The crowd answered him, We have heard out of the law that the Christ abides for ever; and how sayest thou that the Son of man must be lifted up? Who is this, the Son of man?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said to them, Yet a little while is the light amongst you. Walk while ye have the light, that darkness may not overtake you. And he who walks in the darkness does not know where he goes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While ye have the light, believe in the light, that ye may become sons of light. Jesus said these things, and going away hid himself from them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But though he had done so many signs before them, they believed not on him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that the word of the prophet Esaias which he said might be fulfilled, Lord, who has believed our report? and to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On this account they could not believe, because Esaias said again,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He has blinded their eyes and hardened their heart, that they may not see with their eyes, and understand with their heart and be converted, and I should heal them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things said Esaias because he saw his glory and spoke of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Although indeed from among the rulers also many believed on him, but on account of the Pharisees did not confess [him], that they might not be put out of the synagogue:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for they loved glory from men rather than glory fromGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus cried and said, He that believes on me, believes not on me, but on him that sent me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he that beholds me, beholds him that sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am come into the world [as] light, that every one that believes on me may not abide in darkness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if any one hear my words and do not keep [them], I judge him not, for I am not come that I might judge the world, but that I might save the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that rejects me and does not receive my words, has him who judges him: the word which I have spoken, that shall judge him in the last day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have not spoken from myself, but the Father who sent me has himself given me commandment what I should say and what I should speak;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I know that his commandment is life eternal. What therefore I speak, as the Father has said to me, so I speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now before the feast of the passover, Jesus, knowing that his hour had come that he should depart out of this world to the Father, having loved his own who were in the world, loved them to the end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And during supper, the devil having already put it into the heart of Judas [son] of Simon, Iscariote, that he should deliver him up,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Jesus,] knowing that the Father had given him all things into his hands, and that he came out fromGod and was going toGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: rises from supper and lays aside his garments, and having taken a linen towel he girded himself:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then he pours water into the washhand basin, and began to wash the feet of the disciples, and to wipe them with the linen towel with which he was girded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He comes therefore to Simon Peter; and he says to him, Lord, dost thou wash my feet?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to him, What I do thou dost not know now, but thou shalt know hereafter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter says to him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, Unless I wash thee, thou hast not part with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon Peter says to him, Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to him, He that is washed all over needs not to wash save his feet, but is wholly clean; and ye are clean, but not all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he knew him that delivered him up: on account of this he said, Ye are not all clean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore he had washed their feet, and taken his garments, having sat down again, he said to them, Do ye know what I have done to you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye call me the Teacher and the Lord, and ye say well, for I am [so].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I therefore, the Lord and the Teacher, have washed your feet, ye also ought to wash one another's feet;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I have given you an example that, as I have done to you, ye should do also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say to you, The bondman is not greater than his lord, nor the sent greater than he who has sent him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye know these things, blessed are ye if ye do them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I speak not of you all. I know those whom I have chosen; but that the scripture might be fulfilled, He that eats bread with me has lifted up his heel against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I tell you [it] now before it happens, that when it happens, ye may believe that I am [he].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say to you, He who receives whomsoever I shall send receives me; and he that receives me receives him who has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having said these things, Jesus was troubled in spirit, and testified and said, Verily, verily, I say to you, that one of you shall deliver me up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The disciples therefore looked one on another, doubting of whom he spoke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there was at table one of his disciples in the bosom of Jesus, whom Jesus loved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon Peter makes a sign therefore to him to ask who it might be of whom he spoke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, leaning on the breast of Jesus, says to him, Lord, who is it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answers, He it is to whom I, after I have dipped the morsel, give it. And having dipped the morsel, he gives it to Judas [son] of Simon, Iscariote.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, after the morsel, then entered Satan into him. Jesus therefore says to him, What thou doest, do quickly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But none of those at table knew why he said this to him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for some supposed, because Judas had the bag, that Jesus was saying to him, Buy the things of which we have need for the feast; or that he should give something to the poor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having therefore received the morsel, he went out immediately; and it was night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore he was gone out Jesus says, Now is the Son of man glorified, andGod is glorified in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: IfGod be glorified in him,God also shall glorify him in himself, and shall glorify him immediately.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me; and, as I said to the Jews, Where I go ye cannot come, I say to you also now.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A new commandment I give to you, that ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By this shall all know that ye are disciples of mine, if ye have love amongst yourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon Peter says to him, Lord, where goest thou? Jesus answered him, Where I go thou canst not follow me now, but thou shalt follow me after.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter says to him, Lord, why cannot I follow thee now? I will lay down my life for thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answers, Thou wilt lay down thy life for me! Verily, verily, I say to thee, The cock shall not crow till thou hast denied me thrice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let not your heart be troubled; ye believe onGod, believe also on me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In my Father's house there are many abodes; were it not so, I had told you: for I go to prepare you a place;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if I go and shall prepare you a place, I am coming again and shall receive you to myself, that where I am ye also may be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye know where I go, and ye know the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thomas says to him, Lord, we know not where thou goest, and how can we know the way?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to him, I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father unless by me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye had known me, ye would have known also my Father, and henceforth ye know him and have seen him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Philip says to him, Lord, shew us the Father and it suffices us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to him, Am I so long a time with you, and thou hast not known me, Philip? He that has seen me has seen the Father; and how sayest thou, Shew us the Father?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Believest thou not that I [am] in the Father, and that the Father is in me? The words which I speak to you I do not speak from myself; but the Father who abides in me, he does the works.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Believe me that I [am] in the Father and the Father in me; but if not, believe me for the works' sake themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say to you, He that believes on me, the works which I do shall he do also, and he shall do greater than these, because I go to the Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whatsoever ye shall ask in my name, this will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye shall ask anything in my name, I will do it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye love me, keep my commandments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will beg the Father, and he will give you another Comforter, that he may be with you for ever,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it does not see him nor know him; but ye know him, for he abides with you, and shall be in you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will not leave you orphans, I am coming to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet a little and the world sees me no longer; but ye see me; because I live ye also shall live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In that day ye shall know that I [am] in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that has my commandments and keeps them, he it is that loves me; but he that loves me shall be loved by my Father, and I will love him and will manifest myself to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judas, not the Iscariote, says to him, Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself to us and not to the world?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered and said to him, If any one love me, he will keep my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our abode with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that loves me not does not keep my words; and the word which ye hear is not mine, but [that] of the Father who has sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things I have said to you, abiding with you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the Comforter, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and will bring to your remembrance all the things which I have said to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I leave peace with you; I give my peace to you: not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it fear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have heard that I have said unto you, I go away and I am coming to you. If ye loved me ye would rejoice that I go to the Father, for [my] Father is greater than I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now I have told you before it comes to pass, that when it shall have come to pass ye may believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will no longer speak much with you, for the ruler of the world comes, and in me he has nothing;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but that the world may know that I love the Father, and as the Father has commanded me, thus I do. Rise up, let us go hence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As to] every branch in me not bearing fruit, he takes it away; and [as to] every one bearing fruit, he purges it that it may bring forth more fruit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye are already clean by reason of the word which I have spoken to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Abide in me and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself unless it abide in the vine, thus neither [can] ye unless ye abide in me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am the vine, ye [are] the branches. He that abides in me and I in him, he bears much fruit; for without me ye can do nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unless any one abide in me he is cast out as the branch, and is dried up; and they gather them and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will and it shall come to pass to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In this is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit, and ye shall become disciples of mine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As the Father has loved me, I also have loved you: abide in my love.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye shall keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love, as I have kept my Father's commandments and abide in his love.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have spoken these things to you that my joy may be in you, and your joy be full.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is my commandment, that ye love one another, as I have loved you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No one has greater love than this, that one should lay down his life for his friends.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye are my friends if ye practise whatever I command you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I call you no longer bondmen, for the bondman does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you friends, for all things which I have heard of my Father I have made known to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and have set you that ye should go and [that] ye should bear fruit, and [that] your fruit should abide, that whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name he may give you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things I command you, that ye love one another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the world hate you, know that it has hated me before you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye were of the world, the world would love its own; but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, on account of this the world hates you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember the word which I said unto you, The bondman is not greater than his master. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my word, they will keep also yours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they will do all these things to you on account of my name, because they have not known him that sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I had not come and spoken to them, they had not had sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that hates me hates also my Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I had not done among them the works which no other one has done, they had not had sin; but now they have both seen and hated both me and my Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that the word written in their law might be fulfilled, They hated me without a cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who goes forth from with the Father, he shall bear witness concerning me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye too bear witness, because ye are with me from [the] beginning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things I have spoken unto you that ye may not be offended.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall put you out of the synagogues; but the hour is coming that every one who kills you will think to render service toGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and these things they will do because they have not known the Father nor me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I have spoken these things to you, that when their hour shall have come, ye may remember them, that I have said [them] unto you. But I did not say these things unto you from [the] beginning, because I was with you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now I go to him that has sent me, and none of you demands of me, Where goest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But because I have spoken these things to you, sorrow has filled your heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say the truth to you, It is profitable for you that I go away; for if I do not go away, the Comforter will not come to you; but if I go I will send him to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having come, he will bring demonstration to the world, of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of sin, because they do not believe on me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of righteousness, because I go away to [my] Father, and ye behold me no longer;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of judgment, because the ruler of this world is judged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have yet many things to say to you, but ye cannot bear them now.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when he is come, the Spirit of truth, he shall guide you into all the truth: for he shall not speak from himself; but whatsoever he shall hear he shall speak; and he will announce to you what is coming.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall glorify me, for he shall receive of mine and shall announce [it] to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All things that the Father has are mine; on account of this I have said that he receives of mine and shall announce [it] to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A little while and ye do not behold me; and again a little while and ye shall see me, [because I go away to the Father].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Some] of his disciples therefore said to one another, What is this he says to us, A little while and ye do not behold me; and again a little while and ye shall see me, and, Because I go away to the Father?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said therefore, What is this which he says [of] the little while? We do not know [of] what he speaks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus knew therefore that they desired to demand of him, and said to them, Do ye inquire of this among yourselves that I said, A little while and ye do not behold me; and again a little while and ye shall see me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say to you, that ye shall weep and lament, ye, but the world shall rejoice; and ye will be grieved, but your grief shall be turned to joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A woman, when she gives birth to a child, has grief because her hour has come; but when the child is born, she no longer remembers the trouble, on account of the joy that a man has been born into the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye now therefore have grief; but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no one takes from you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in that day ye shall demand nothing of me: verily, verily, I say to you, Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hitherto ye have asked nothing in my name: ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things I have spoken to you in allegories; the hour is coming that I will no longer speak to you in allegories, but will declare to you openly concerning the Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In that day ye shall ask in my name; and I say not to you that I will demand of the Father for you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the Father himself has affection for you, because ye have had affection for me, and have believed that I came out fromGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I came out from the Father and have come into the world; again, I leave the world and go to the Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His disciples say to him, Lo, now thou speakest openly and utterest no allegory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now we know that thou knowest all things, and hast not need that any one should demand of thee. By this we believe that thou art come fromGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, [the] hour is coming, and has come, that ye shall be scattered, each to his own, and shall leave me alone; and [yet] I am not alone, for the Father is with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things have I spoken to you that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye have tribulation; but be of good courage: I have overcome the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things Jesus spoke, and lifted up his eyes to heaven and said, Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that thy Son may glorify thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as thou hast given him authority over all flesh, that [as to] all that thou hast given to him, he should give them life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the eternal life, that they should know thee, the only trueGod, and Jesus Christ whom thou hast sent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have glorified thee on the earth, I have completed the work which thou gavest me that I should do it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and now glorify me, thou Father, along with thyself, with the glory which I had along with thee before the world was.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have manifested thy name to the men whom thou gavest me out of the world. They were thine, and thou gavest them me, and they have kept thy word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now they have known that all things that thou hast given me are of thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the words which thou hast given me I have given them, and they have received [them], and have known truly that I came out from thee, and have believed that thou sentest me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I demand concerning them; I do not demand concerning the world, but concerning those whom thou hast given me, for they are thine,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (and all that is mine is thine, and [all] that is thine mine,) and I am glorified in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I am no longer in the world, and these are in the world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep them in thy name which thou hast given me, that they may be one as we.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I was with them I kept them in thy name; those thou hast given me I have guarded, and not one of them has perished, but the son of perdition, that the scripture might be fulfilled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now I come to thee. And these things I speak in the world, that they may have my joy fulfilled in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have given them thy word, and the world has hated them, because they are not of the world, as I am not of the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I do not demand that thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that thou shouldest keep them out of evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are not of the world, as I am not of the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sanctify them by the truth: thy word is truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As thou hast sent me into the world, I also have sent them into the world;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I sanctify myself for them, that they also may be sanctified by truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I do not demand for these only, but also for those who believe on me through their word;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they may be all one, as thou, Father, [art] in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us, that the world may believe that thou hast sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the glory which thou hast given me I have given them, that they may be one, as we are one;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I in them and thou in me, that they may be perfected into one [and] that the world may know that thou hast sent me, and [that] thou hast loved them as thou hast loved me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Father, [as to] those whom thou hast given me, I desire that where I am they also may be with me, that they may behold my glory which thou hast given me, for thou lovedst me before [the] foundation of [the] world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Righteous Father, — and the world has not known thee, but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have made known to them thy name, and will make [it] known; that the love with which thou hast loved me may be in them and I in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus, having said these things, went out with his disciples beyond the torrent Cedron, where was a garden, into which he entered, he and his disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judas also, who delivered him up, knew the place, because Jesus was often there, in company with his disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judas therefore, having got the band, and officers of the chief priests and Pharisees, comes there with lanterns and torches and weapons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore, knowing all things that were coming upon him, went forth and said to them, Whom seek ye?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They answered him, Jesus the Nazaraean. Jesus says to them, I am [he]. And Judas also, who delivered him up, stood with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore he said to them, I am [he], they went away backward and fell to the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He demanded of them therefore again, Whom seek ye? And they said, Jesus the Nazaraean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, I told you that I am [he]: if therefore ye seek me, let these go away;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that the word might be fulfilled which he spoke, [As to] those whom thou hast given me, I have not lost one of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon Peter therefore, having a sword, drew it, and smote the bondman of the high priest and cut off his right ear; and the bondman's name was Malchus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore said to Peter, Put the sword into the sheath; the cup which the Father has given me, shall I not drink it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The band therefore, and the chiliarch, and the officers of the Jews, took Jesus and bound him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they led him away to Annas first; for he was father-in-law to Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But it was Caiaphas who counselled the Jews that it was better that one man should perish for the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Simon Peter followed Jesus, and the other disciple. But that disciple was known to the high priest, and went in with Jesus into the palace of the high priest;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but Peter stood at the door without. The other disciple therefore, who was known to the high priest, went out and spoke to the porteress and brought in Peter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The maid therefore, who was porteress, says to Peter, Art thou also of the disciples of this man? He says, I am not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the bondmen and officers, having made a fire of coals (for it was cold), stood and warmed themselves; and Peter was standing with them and warming himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The high priest therefore demanded of Jesus concerning his disciples and concerning his doctrine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered him, I spoke openly to the world; I taught always in [the] synagogue and in the temple, where all the Jews come together, and in secret I have spoken nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why demandest thou of me? Demand of those who have heard, what I have spoken to them; behold, they know what I have said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as he said these things, one of the officers who stood by gave a blow on the face to Jesus, saying, Answerest thou the high priest thus?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered him, If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil; but if well, why smitest thou me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Annas [then] had sent him bound to Caiaphas the high priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Simon Peter was standing and warming himself. They said therefore to him, Art thou also of his disciples? He denied, and said, I am not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One of the bondmen of the high priest, who was kinsman of him whose ear Peter cut off, says, Did not I see thee in the garden with him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter denied therefore again, and immediately [the] cock crew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They lead therefore Jesus from Caiaphas to the praetorium; and it was early morn. And they entered not into the praetorium, that they might not be defiled, but eat the passover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate therefore went out to them and said, What accusation do ye bring against this man?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They answered and said to him, If this [man] were not an evildoer, we should not have delivered him up to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate therefore said to them, Take him, ye, and judge him according to your law. The Jews therefore said to him, It is not permitted to us to put any one to death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled which he spoke, signifying what death he should die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate therefore entered again into the praetorium and called Jesus, and said to him, Thou art the king of the Jews?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered [him], Dost thou say this of thyself, or have others said it to thee concerning me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate answered, Am I a Jew? Thy nation and the chief priests have delivered thee up to me: what hast thou done?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, My kingdom is not of this world; if my kingdom were of this world, my servants had fought that I might not be delivered up to the Jews; but now my kingdom is not from hence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate therefore said to him, Thou art then a king? Jesus answered, Thou sayest [it], that I am a king. I have been born for this, and for this I have come into the world, that I might bear witness to the truth. Every one that is of the truth hears my voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate says to him, What is truth? And having said this he went out again to the Jews, and says to them, I find no fault whatever in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye have a custom that I release [some] one to you at the passover; will ye therefore that I release unto you the king of the Jews?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They cried therefore again all, saying, Not this [man], but Barabbas. Now Barabbas was a robber.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Pilate therefore took Jesus and scourged [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the soldiers having plaited a crown of thorns put it on his head, and put a purple robe on him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and came to him and said, Hail, king of the Jews! and gave him blows on the face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate went out again and says to them, Lo, I bring him out to you, that ye may know that I find in him no fault whatever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (Jesus therefore went forth without, wearing the crown of thorn, and the purple robe.) And he says to them, Behold the man!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore the chief priests and the officers saw him they cried out saying, Crucify, crucify [him]. Pilate says to them, Take him ye and crucify [him], for I find no fault in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews answered him, We have a law, and according to [our] law he ought to die, because he made himself Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When Pilate therefore heard this word, he was the rather afraid,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and went into the praetorium again and says to Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate therefore says to him, Speakest thou not to me? Dost thou not know that I have authority to release thee and have authority to crucify thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, Thou hadst no authority whatever against me if it were not given to thee from above. On this account he that has delivered me up to thee has [the] greater sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From this time Pilate sought to release him; but the Jews cried out saying, If thou releasest this [man], thou art not a friend to Caesar. Every one making himself a king speaks against Caesar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate therefore, having heard these words, led Jesus out and sat down upon [the] judgment-seat, at a place called Pavement, but in Hebrew Gabbatha;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (now it was [the] preparation of the passover; it was about the sixth hour;) and he says to the Jews, Behold your king!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they cried out, Take [him] away, take [him] away, crucify him. Pilate says to them, Shall I crucify your king? The chief priests answered, We have no king but Caesar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then therefore he delivered him up to them, that he might be crucified; and they took Jesus and led him away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went out, bearing his cross, to the place called [place] of a skull, which is called in Hebrew, Golgotha;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: where they crucified him, and with him two others, [one] on this side, and [one] on that, and Jesus in the middle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate wrote a title also and put it on the cross. But there was written: Jesus the Nazaraean, the King of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This title therefore many of the Jews read, for the place of the city where Jesus was crucified was near; and it was written in Hebrew, Greek, Latin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The chief priests of the Jews therefore said to Pilate, Do not write, The king of the Jews, but that he said, I am king of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate answered, What I have written, I have written.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The soldiers therefore, when they had crucified Jesus, took his clothes, and made four parts, to each soldier a part, and the body-coat; but the body-coat was seamless, woven through the whole from the top.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They said therefore to one another, Let us not rend it, but let us cast lots for it, whose it shall be; that the scripture might be fulfilled which says, They parted my garments among themselves, and on my vesture they cast lots. The soldiers therefore did these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by the cross of Jesus stood his mother, and the sister of his mother, Mary the [wife] of Clopas, and Mary of Magdala.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore, seeing his mother, and the disciple standing by, whom he loved, says to his mother, Woman, behold thy son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he says unto the disciple, Behold thy mother. And from that hour the disciple took her to his own home.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After this, Jesus, knowing that all things were now finished, that the scripture might be fulfilled, says, I thirst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was a vessel therefore there full of vinegar, and having filled a sponge with vinegar, and putting hyssop round it, they put it up to his mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore Jesus had received the vinegar, he said, It is finished; and having bowed his head, he delivered up his spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Jews therefore, that the bodies might not remain on the cross on the sabbath, for it was [the] preparation, (for the day of that sabbath was a great [day],) demanded of Pilate that their legs might be broken and they taken away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The soldiers therefore came and broke the legs of the first and of the other that had been crucified with him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but coming to Jesus, when they saw that he was already dead they did not break his legs,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately there came out blood and water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he who saw it bears witness, and his witness is true, and he knows that he says true that ye also may believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For these things took place that the scripture might be fulfilled, Not a bone of him shall be broken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again another scripture says, They shall look on him whom they pierced.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after these things Joseph of Arimathaea, who was a disciple of Jesus, but secretly through fear of the Jews, demanded of Pilate that he might take the body of Jesus: and Pilate allowed it. He came therefore and took away the body of Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nicodemus also, who at first came to Jesus by night, came, bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred pounds [weight].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They took therefore the body of Jesus and bound it up in linen with the spices, as it is the custom with the Jews to prepare for burial.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there was in the place where he had been crucified a garden, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had ever been laid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There therefore, on account of the preparation of the Jews, because the tomb was near, they laid Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the first [day] of the week Mary of Magdala comes in early morn to the tomb, while it was still dark, and sees the stone taken away from the tomb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She runs therefore and comes to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple, to whom Jesus was attached, and says to them, They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we know not where they have laid him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter therefore went forth, and the other disciple, and came to the tomb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the two ran together, and the other disciple ran forward faster than Peter, and came first to the tomb,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and stooping down he sees the linen cloths lying; he did not however go in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon Peter therefore comes, following him, and entered into the tomb, and sees the linen cloths lying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the handkerchief which was upon his head, not lying with the linen cloths, but folded up in a distinct place by itself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then entered in therefore the other disciple also who came first to the tomb, and he saw and believed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for they had not yet known the scripture, that he must rise from among [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The disciples therefore went away again to their own home.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Mary stood at the tomb weeping without. As therefore she wept, she stooped down into the tomb,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and beholds two angels sitting in white [garments], one at the head and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they say to her, Woman, why dost thou weep? She says to them, Because they have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having said these things she turned backward and beholds Jesus standing [there], and knew not that it was Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to her, Woman, why dost thou weep? Whom seekest thou? She, supposing that it was the gardener, says to him, Sir, if thou hast borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to her, Mary. She, turning round, says to him in Hebrew, Rabboni, which means Teacher.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to her, Touch me not, for I have not yet ascended to my Father; but go to my brethren and say to them, I ascend to my Father and your Father, and [to] myGod and yourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mary of Magdala comes bringing word to the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and [that] he had said these things to her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore it was evening on that day, which was the first [day] of the week, and the doors shut where the disciples were, through fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the midst, and says to them, Peace [be] to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having said this, he shewed to them his hands and his side. The disciples rejoiced therefore, having seen the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Jesus] said therefore again to them, Peace [be] to you: as the Father sent me forth, I also send you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having said this, he breathed into [them], and says to them, Receive [the] Holy Spirit:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whose soever sins ye remit, they are remitted to them; whose soever [sins] ye retain, they are retained.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The other disciples therefore said to him, We have seen the Lord. But he said to them, Unless I see in his hands the mark of the nails, and put my finger into the mark of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And eight days after, his disciples were again within, and Thomas with them. Jesus comes, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst and said, Peace [be] to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he says to Thomas, Bring thy finger here and see my hands; and bring thy hand and put it into my side; and be not unbelieving, but believing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thomas answered and said to him, My Lord and myGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to him, Because thou hast seen me thou hast believed: blessed they who have not seen and have believed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many other signs therefore also Jesus did before his disciples, which are not written in this book;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but these are written that ye may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son ofGod, and that believing ye might have life in his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After these things Jesus manifested himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias. And he manifested [himself] thus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael who was of Cana of Galilee, and the [sons] of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon Peter says to them, I go to fish. They say to him, We also come with thee. They went forth, and went on board, and that night took nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And early morn already breaking, Jesus stood on the shore; the disciples however did not know that it was Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus therefore says to them, Children, have ye anything to eat? They answered him, No.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Cast the net at the right side of the ship and ye will find. They cast therefore, and they could no longer draw it, from the multitude of fishes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved says to Peter, It is the Lord. Simon Peter therefore, having heard that it was the Lord, girded his overcoat [on him] (for he was naked), and cast himself into the sea;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the other disciples came in the small boat, for they were not far from the land, but somewhere about two hundred cubits, dragging the net of fishes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore they went out on the land, they see a fire of coals there, and fish laid on it, and bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to them, Bring of the fishes which ye have now taken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon Peter went up and drew the net to the land full of great fishes, a hundred and fifty-three; and though there were so many, the net was not rent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to them, Come [and] dine. But none of the disciples dared inquire of him, Who art thou? knowing that it was the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus comes and takes the bread and gives it to them, and the fish in like manner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is already the third time that Jesus had been manifested to the disciples, being risen from among [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore they had dined, Jesus says to Simon Peter, Simon, [son] of Jonas, lovest thou me more than these? He says to him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I am attached to thee. He says to him, Feed my lambs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He says to him again a second time, Simon, [son] of Jonas, lovest thou me? He says to him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I am attached to thee. He says to him, Shepherd my sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He says to him the third time, Simon, [son] of Jonas, art thou attached to me? Peter was grieved because he said to him the third time, Art thou attached to me? and said to him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I am attached to thee. Jesus says to him, Feed my sheep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily, verily, I say to thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst where thou desiredst; but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and bring thee where thou dost not desire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said this signifying by what death he should glorifyGod. And having said this, he says to him, Follow me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter, turning round, sees the disciple whom Jesus loved following, who also leaned at supper on his breast, and said, Lord, who is it that delivers thee up?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter, seeing him, says to Jesus, Lord, and what [of] this [man]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to him, If I will that he abide until I come, what [is that] to thee? Follow thou me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This word therefore went out among the brethren, That disciple does not die. And Jesus did not say to him, He does not die; but, If I will that he abide until I come, what [is that] to thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the disciple who bears witness concerning these things, and who has written these things; and we know that his witness is true.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there are also many other things which Jesus did, the which if they were written one by one, I suppose that not even the world itself would contain the books written.
